title: Video
description: Video provides embedded playback with optional browser controls or a custom control layer that can expose play, progress, volume, fullscreen, speed, quality, skip, and picture-in-picture actions.
intro: Use `ui-video` when the product needs embedded media playback inside dashboards, learning surfaces, previews, or documentation. Start with browser controls for generic playback. Switch to custom controls only when the surrounding experience needs a more tailored control set.

featureSections.title: Basic playback
featureSections.description: The simplest and safest pattern is a normal embedded video with browser controls and a poster image.

featureSections.title: Browser versus custom controls
featureSections.description: Use browser controls for generic media playback. Use custom controls only when the product needs a more tailored playback surface.

featureSections.title: Playback behavior
featureSections.description: Autoplay, loop, and muted behavior can change the role of the component from passive preview to more ambient media playback.

featureSections.title: Size variants
featureSections.description: Size affects how prominent the player feels in cards, side panels, and full content areas.

featureSections.title: Advanced actions
featureSections.description: Speed, quality, skip, fullscreen, and picture-in-picture controls make sense when the content is actively watched rather than just previewed.

featureSections.title: Media panel composition
featureSections.description: A practical video example should show the player inside a real content or learning panel rather than as a naked configuration sample.

accessibility.title: Playback surface
accessibility.body:
`ui-video` wraps a native HTML `video` element and layers optional custom playback controls around it.

| Element | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| video element | native video semantics |
| browser controls mode | relies on native browser media controls |
| custom controls mode | exposes explicit buttons and sliders for playback actions |

accessibility.title: Keyboard and control discoverability
accessibility.body:
Keyboard behavior depends on the enabled controls and the browser's media support.

| Mode | Interaction model |
| --- | --- |
| native controls | browser-defined keyboard and screen-reader behavior |
| custom buttons and sliders | standard button and slider keyboard behavior |
| hidden or optional actions | should be enabled only when users genuinely need them |

accessibility.title: Usage guidance
accessibility.body: Prefer native browser controls when accessibility and familiarity matter more than custom chrome. If you enable custom actions like quality or playback speed, make sure the surrounding product truly supports and explains them.

apiSections.title: VideoComponent - ui-video
apiSections.lead: Standalone embedded video player with optional custom control overlay. It can expose browser controls or a richer custom playback surface depending on the product need.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Repeats playback after the video ends.
Uses the browser's native video controls.
Toggles core custom controls when native controls are disabled.
Enables more advanced custom playback actions.
Skip distance for forward and backward controls.
Displayed quality menu options when the quality action is enabled.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emits when metadata such as duration becomes available.

paragraphs: Custom controls are strongest in product-specific media surfaces. For generic content playback, native browser controls usually remain the simpler choice.
Quality switching UI is presentational unless the surrounding application also swaps the actual media source or integrates adaptive streaming logic.
blocks.title: Notes
